title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: After 35 years, Ig Nobel ceremony to leave the U.S.
items.description: Finances and Trump policies underlaid the decision to move the satirical science event to Switzerland

items.title: ‘Tour de force’ mouse study shows a gut microbe can promote memory loss
items.description: Research suggests the microbiome may contribute to cognitive decline—but its relevance in humans is unclear

items.title: A genetic trick helps this all-female fish species escape evolutionary doom
items.description: The Amazon molly, which reproduces asexually, has survived—and thrived—at least 10 times longer than predicted by evolutionary theory

items.title: Universe’s brightest stellar explosions may be powered by highly magnetic neutron stars
items.description: Peculiar bumps in light from supercharged supernovae point to a stellar remnant surrounded by a wobbling disk of matter

items.title: Why the Energy Department’s science labs will spearhead the federal push into AI
items.description: DOE’s national labs’ huge facilities, data flows, and computing resources are a natural fit for the Genesis Mission

items.title: Viagra compound may hold promise for treating fatal genetic disease
items.description: Animal studies and preliminary human data support the safety of sildenafil in Leigh syndrome, but the jury’s out on whether it’s effective

items.title: And the winner of <cite>Science</cite>’s 2026 ‘Dance Your Ph.D.’ contest is ...
items.description: A physicist and professional dancer takes home the grand prize, and <cite>Science</cite> awards its first trophy for an AI-assisted dance concept

items.title: How bumble bees survive days underwater without drowning
items.description: Insects can stay alive for up to a week in flooded nests, thanks to several tricks

items.title: White House plan to break up iconic U.S. climate lab moves forward
items.description: Bidders have lined up to take over pieces of the National Center for Atmospheric Research

items.title: Ancient Peruvians transported live parrots across the Andes
items.description: Pre-Inca elites wore headdresses made of feathers from Amazonian macaws
